vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate based on visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/unidentifiable. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not visible as healthy specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable biological presence, though severely compromised.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No mint-like foliage detected.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos specimen identified by leaf morphology; White Rabbit anchor present.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No silver-type foliage detected.

plant_audit.P3: Single leaf visible; shows signs of chlorosis and turgor loss.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Significant eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 pot area; indicates potential soil amendment or pest deterrent attempt.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: Desk surface is clear of organic debris; lighting is insufficient for photosynthesis.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: From EARLIEST to CURRENT, the P3 specimen has undergone progressive postural collapse. The single leaf has transitioned from a semi-upright position to a horizontal, wilted state. No new growth has occurred in any pot over the 5-day observation window.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The lack of chlorophyll-rich foliage and the persistent wilting of the P3 specimen suggest severe dehydration or root-zone failure. The environment is currently incapable of supporting the registered species.
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments (likely calcium supplementation attempt); absence of all registered plants except one struggling Pothos leaf.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a chronological audit of the provided image stack. The initial state shows a collection of pots with minimal biological activity. Over the 5-day sequence, the P3 Pothos leaf has lost turgor pressure and is now in a state of collapse. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) remain devoid of visible plant life, confirming a systemic loss of the intended biome. The environment is currently hostile to the registered flora.
